description: Interactive winnowing (blowing-air separation) lab. A farmer pours a tray of a heavy+light mixture (wheat grains + husk) into the wind; the student predicts which part blows away, picks an air strength, and watches realistic falling/pouring physics separate the heavy grains (which fall into a rounded mound) from the light husk (which is carried downwind into a thin scatter).

width.description: Maximum component width in pixels (component is fully responsive down to 320px)

height.description: Suggested height in pixels; the stage keeps a 760:480 aspect ratio and scales to fit

initialMode.description: Starting mode. This tool is a predict-observe-explain (POE) activity and runs primarily in hands_on.

enabledModes.description: Which modes to enable. The activity is a single hands_on POE flow.

showStepIndicator.description: Show/hide the phase progress indicator (predict -> ready -> blowing -> result)

filterSteps.description: Array of phase IDs to show

animationSpeed.description: Multiplier on the falling/pouring physics speed (e.g. 0.5 = slow motion, 2 = fast)

autoPlayDuration.description: Auto-advance delay in ms (0 = disabled; the activity is student-driven)

gradeLevel.description: Tunes tone, font sizes, touch-target sizes, story framing, and the celebration intensity (confetti/stars). 6 = most playful, 8 = most concise.

additionalProps.description: Tool-specific dynamic content passed by the agent to configure the winnowing scenario

material.description: Which mixture to separate. 'wheat' = wheat grains + light husk.

lockMaterial.description: If true, hides the material switch so the student cannot change the mixture (keeps focus on a single scenario).

defaultStrength.description: Pre-selected wind strength. gentle = poor separation (husk left behind), medium = clean winnowing, strong = all husk blown far but a little grain wasted.

heavyCount.description: Number of heavy pieces (grains) poured. Controls the size of the grain mound.

lightCount.description: Number of light pieces (husk/skins) poured. Controls how much chaff is carried away.

hands_on.description: Predict-Observe-Explain winnowing: predict, choose wind, blow, then review the result.

3.title: Blow the air and observe the separation

toolCallExamples.title: Winnowing Wheat
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Farmers use wind to separate heavy grain from light husk. Try it!

toolCallExamples.title: Why a Gentle Breeze Isn't Enough
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: A weak breeze can't carry every bit of husk away. Watch what stays behind.

toolCallExamples.title: Too Strong a Gust Wastes Grain
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: A strong gust removes all the husk, but a little grain gets blown off the pile too.

toolCallExamples.title: Winnowing in Slow Motion
toolCallExamples.instructions_for_student: Watch closely in slow motion: heavy wheat grains drop straight down, light husk drifts away.

errorHandling: Empty additionalProps: shows the default wheat + steady-wind scenario.
